feedback.text: Hi. I want to say, that Digital Fairness Act is a great thing and that it's very essential in our times. I would like to give a little suggestion: matter of ownership of modern games is a topic worth adding to as a regulation in that initiative. Now publishers can just take away access to a game they sold at any time for any or even no reason at all, just because of the design they implemented do the game. It's not a fair practice and a gray area that need to be regulated. Of course no one expect them to lose money by maintaining service that isn't profitable. But they need to have a obligation to ensure other means or proper tools for owners of the game to have access to it or for the game to be playable as before. Please take a look at that matter, because it's very important to many of EU citizens as practices of game publishers are getting more and more impudent, unfair and aggressive. There are many more malpractices in other maters, like monetization and microtranzaction, but topic of destroying games is a natural for for DFA. Thank you. :)
